scope: fresh arc after exhausted Session 29/30/31 queue

items.why: The repo already has a tested shared modal focus trap, and gameFlow uses it, but the first-run tutorial overlay remains a hand-rolled dialog. That leaves keyboard focus and Escape behavior inconsistent on the first player-facing onboarding moment.
items.recipe: Import openModal/closeModal in public/lib/tutorialCampaign.js, pair every tutorial exit with closeModal before removing the overlay, open the trap after each step render, and add focused static coverage in test/browser-wiring.test.js.

L1.recipe: Trap tutorial focus and cover wiring.

L2.recipe: Also add a DOM-level fake test for Escape.

L3.recipe: Adopt the modal manager across every remaining overlay.

items.implementation: public/lib/tutorialCampaign.js now imports the shared modal manager, opens the focus trap after each tutorial step render, closes any previous trap before rerendering, and closes the trap before skip/complete removal. test/browser-wiring.test.js asserts this wiring remains present.

skipped.reason: Rejected on live evidence: Session 29/30 audit queue is exhausted in context/TASK_BOARD.md and .cache/genius-list.json after Session 31 repair. Reopening it would be a false-positive queue regression.
